questions.text: How many players are on a soccer/football team on the field?
questions.explanation: Each team has 11 players on the field in association football.

questions.text: Wimbledon is the oldest tennis tournament in the world.
questions.explanation: Wimbledon was first held in 1877 and is the oldest major tennis tournament.

questions.text: In which country are the Olympics 2024 being held?
questions.explanation: Paris, France hosted the 2024 Summer Olympics.

questions.text: Who won the men's singles at Australian Open 2024?
questions.explanation: Jannik Sinner won the Australian Open 2024 men's singles title.

questions.text: Match sports with their governing bodies:
questions.explanation: These are the international governing bodies for each sport.

questions.text: The FIFA World Cup is held every 4 years.
questions.explanation: The FIFA World Cup tournament occurs every 4 years.

questions.text: Which team won the ICC Women's World Cup 2023?
questions.explanation: Spain won the FIFA Women's World Cup 2023.

questions.text: Which sports are played with a ball? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Cricket, badminton, and tennis are ball sports. Chess and swimming don't use balls.

questions.text: Assertion: Rohan Bopanna became world No. 1 in doubles tennis. Reason: He was the oldest person to achieve this ranking.
questions.explanation: Both statements are accurate regarding Rohan Bopanna's achievement.

questions.text: The Wimbledon tennis tournament is played on which surface?
questions.explanation: Wimbledon is famous for its grass courts, the only Grand Slam on grass.

questions.text: Which country won the ICC Men's Cricket World Cup in 2023?
questions.explanation: Australia defeated India in the final to win the ICC Men's Cricket World Cup 2023.

questions.text: Michael Phelps holds the record for the most Olympic gold medals won by an individual.
questions.explanation: Michael Phelps won 23 Olympic gold medals during his swimming career, the most by any athlete.

questions.text: The scoring term 'birdie' is primarily used in the sport of ______.
questions.explanation: In golf, a birdie means scoring one stroke under par on a hole.

questions.text: Which of these players have won the prestigious FIFA Ballon d'Or multiple times? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Messi, Ronaldo, and Platini have all won multiple Ballon d'Or awards. Neymar and Rooney have not.

questions.text: Assertion: Test cricket is considered the longest format of the game. Reason: A standard Test match is scheduled to be played over five consecutive days.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct, and the five-day duration is exactly why it is the longest format.

questions.text: Match the legendary athletes to their primary sport:
questions.explanation: These athletes are considered among the greatest of all time in their respective sports.

questions.text: In standard basketball rules, how many points is a successful free throw worth?
questions.explanation: A free throw in basketball is awarded after a foul and is worth 1 point.

questions.text: A standard professional baseball game consists of exactly 9 innings.
questions.explanation: Major League Baseball games are played over 9 innings, unless there is a tie requiring extra innings.

questions.text: The official national winter sport of Canada is ice ______.
questions.explanation: Ice hockey is deeply ingrained in Canadian culture and is their official national winter sport.

questions.text: Which of the following events are part of a standard Men's Decathlon? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: The Decathlon consists of 10 track and field events, including the 100m, long jump, and shot put, but not swimming or gymnastics.

questions.text: Assertion: Simone Biles is widely regarded as one of the greatest gymnasts of all time. Reason: She has won the most World Championship medals in gymnastics history.
questions.explanation: Both statements are true. Her record-breaking medal count is a primary reason for her GOAT status in gymnastics.

questions.text: Match the Grand Slam tennis tournaments with their traditional host cities:
questions.explanation: These are the permanent host cities for three of the four tennis Grand Slams.

questions.text: Who holds the current world record for the men's 100m sprint?
questions.explanation: Usain Bolt set the world record of 9.58 seconds at the 2009 World Athletics Championships in Berlin.

questions.text: The Tour de France is an annual multi-stage bicycle race primarily held in France.
questions.explanation: It is the most prestigious and difficult bicycle race in the world, held annually since 1903.

questions.text: An official marathon race has a standard distance of exactly ______ miles.
questions.explanation: The official distance of a marathon is 26.2 miles, or 42.195 kilometers.

questions.text: Which of the following national teams have won the FIFA Men's World Cup? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Brazil, Germany, and Italy have all won multiple World Cups. The Netherlands and Belgium have never won.

questions.text: Assertion: The yellow jersey (maillot jaune) in the Tour de France is highly coveted. Reason: It is worn by the rider who is the current overall time leader of the race.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct, and being the overall time leader is the exact reason the jersey is awarded.

questions.text: Match the NBA franchises to their home cities:
questions.explanation: These are three of the most historic and successful franchises in NBA history.

questions.text: Which piece in chess is generally considered the most powerful due to its movement capabilities?
questions.explanation: The Queen is the most powerful piece as it can move any number of squares vertically, horizontally, or diagonally.

questions.text: In volleyball, a team is allowed a maximum of three touches to return the ball over the net.
questions.explanation: A team can touch the ball up to three times (usually a bump, set, and spike) before it must cross the net.

questions.text: The Ryder Cup is a famous biennial men's team competition in the sport of ______.
questions.explanation: The Ryder Cup pits teams of professional golfers from Europe against a team from the United States.

questions.text: Which of the following are official strokes used in competitive Olympic swimming? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Butterfly, backstroke, breaststroke, and freestyle are the four official competitive swimming strokes.

questions.text: Assertion: Formula 1 cars are designed with large aerodynamic wings. Reason: The wings generate downforce to push the car into the track, increasing cornering speeds and traction.
questions.explanation: Both statements are true. Aerodynamic downforce is critical for modern F1 cars to take corners at high speeds.

questions.text: Match the martial arts disciplines to their country of origin:
questions.explanation: These martial arts are deeply rooted in the history and culture of their respective originating nations.

questions.text: How many pockets are there on a standard snooker or pool table?
questions.explanation: There are six pockets: one in each of the four corners, and one in the middle of each of the two long cushions.

questions.text: The Thomas Cup is a major international team tournament in men's badminton.
questions.explanation: The Thomas Cup is the premier international men's team championship in badminton, analogous to the Davis Cup in tennis.

questions.text: In ten-pin bowling, scoring three consecutive strikes is commonly known as a ______.
questions.explanation: The term 'turkey' is a long-standing bowling slang for three strikes in a row.

questions.text: Which of these athletes are famous for their careers in Formula 1 racing? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Hamilton, Verstappen, and Alonso are F1 World Champions. Rossi is a MotoGP legend, and Federer plays tennis.

questions.text: Assertion: A 'hat-trick' in football refers to a single player scoring three goals in one game. Reason: The term originated from a 19th-century British cricket tradition of presenting a hat to a bowler who took three wickets with three consecutive deliveries.
questions.explanation: Both statements are true, and the origin in cricket explains how the term made its way into other sports like football and hockey.

questions.text: Match the famous sports trophies to the sport they belong to:
questions.explanation: These are the pinnacle championship trophies for the NHL, NFL, and the England-Australia Test cricket series, respectively.

questions.text: The 'Super Bowl' is the annual championship game of which professional sports league?
questions.explanation: The Super Bowl is the championship game of the National Football League (NFL).

questions.text: In rugby union, a successfully scored 'try' is worth 5 points.
questions.explanation: A try, scored by grounding the ball in the opponent's in-goal area, awards 5 points to the attacking team.

questions.text: The maximum possible break (score in a single visit to the table) without fouls in a standard game of snooker is ______.
questions.explanation: A maximum break involves potting all 15 reds with 15 blacks, followed by all six colors in sequence, totaling 147 points.

questions.text: Which of the following sports are featured in the Winter Olympic Games? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Figure skating, curling, and snowboarding are Winter Olympic sports. Surfing and beach volleyball are in the Summer Games.

questions.text: Assertion: The sport of table tennis has been historically dominated by Asian countries at the Olympics. Reason: China has won the vast majority of all available gold medals in Olympic table tennis history.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. China's sheer dominance in the sport validates the assertion.

questions.text: Match the soccer (football) positions to their primary on-field role:
pairs: Stops the ball from entering the net
questions.explanation: These are the fundamental responsibilities of the basic positions in soccer.

questions.text: The 'All Blacks' rugby team, known for performing the Haka before matches, represents which country?
questions.explanation: The New Zealand national rugby union team performs the traditional Māori Haka to challenge their opponents.

questions.text: In field hockey, players are allowed to use both the flat side and the rounded side of their stick to hit the ball.
questions.explanation: Players are only permitted to use the flat side of the stick; using the rounded back side is a foul.

questions.text: The definitive winning condition in a game of chess, where the opponent's king is inescapably under attack, is called ______.
questions.explanation: Checkmate ends the game immediately, declaring the attacking player the winner.

questions.text: Which of these Grand Slam tennis tournaments are played on hard courts? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: The Australian Open and US Open are played on hard courts. The French Open is on clay, and Wimbledon is on grass. The Davis Cup is not a Grand Slam tournament.

questions.text: In professional golf, what piece of clothing is traditionally awarded to the winner of the Masters Tournament?
questions.explanation: The iconic Green Jacket has been awarded to the winner of the Masters Tournament at Augusta National since 1949.

questions.text: In baseball, a 'perfect game' occurs when a pitcher prevents any opposing player from reaching base during the entire game.
questions.explanation: A perfect game means no hits, no walks, and no errors allowing a runner on base for all nine innings (27 batters).

questions.text: The projectile hit back and forth over the net in a game of badminton is officially called a ______.
questions.explanation: Also known informally as a 'birdie', the shuttlecock is uniquely designed to be aerodynamically stable.

questions.text: Which of the following apparatuses are used in Women's Artistic Gymnastics? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Women compete on vault, uneven bars, balance beam, and floor exercise. Pommel horse and still rings are for men.

questions.text: Match the prominent sports tournament to its corresponding sport:
questions.explanation: These are pinnacle tournaments in international football, European rugby, and American baseball.

questions.text: Assertion: South Korea has historically dominated Olympic archery. Reason: Archery is heavily integrated into their school sports programs and highly funded by national corporations.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. Deep infrastructure and corporate sponsorship have made South Korea an archery powerhouse.

questions.text: Which legendary boxer was famous for the quote 'Float like a butterfly, sting like a bee'?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ali used this famous phrase to describe his fast-moving, hard-hitting boxing style.

questions.text: A standard ice hockey puck is made entirely out of vulcanized rubber.
questions.explanation: Vulcanized rubber is used because it can withstand massive impacts and slide smoothly on the ice when frozen.

questions.text: The marathon race gets its name from a legendary run by a messenger originating from a battlefield in ancient ______.
questions.explanation: The race is named after the Greek soldier Pheidippides, who reportedly ran from the Battle of Marathon to Athens.

questions.text: Which of the following are events in a standard modern Triathlon? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: A standard triathlon is a continuous endurance race consisting of swimming, cycling, and running.

questions.text: Match the scoring terminology to its respective sport:
questions.explanation: These are distinct and famous terms used for scoring or dismissing players in these sports.

questions.text: The famous multi-stage bicycle race known as the 'Giro d'Italia' is held primarily in which country?
questions.explanation: The Giro d'Italia is one of cycling's prestigious three Grand Tours, alongside the Tour de France and Vuelta a España.

questions.text: In a standard game of water polo, each team has seven players in the water at one time.
questions.explanation: A team consists of six outfield players and one goalkeeper in the water.

questions.text: The padded surface on which Olympic wrestling and judo matches take place is called a ______.
questions.explanation: Wrestling and judo both take place on specialized, shock-absorbing mats to protect the athletes.

questions.text: Assertion: The Decision Review System (DRS) was introduced in cricket to assist on-field umpires. Reason: It uses technologies like ball-tracking and edge-detection to overturn clear errors made by the umpires.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. DRS utilizes Hawkeye, UltraEdge, and Snickometer to ensure fair play.

questions.text: Which Formula 1 constructor's team logo features a famous 'prancing horse'?
questions.explanation: Scuderia Ferrari is the oldest and most successful team in F1, represented by the iconic 'Cavallino Rampante'.

questions.text: A standard table tennis ball is solid all the way through to give it more weight.
questions.explanation: Table tennis balls are completely hollow and very lightweight, made of celluloid or similar plastic material.

questions.text: The traditional white uniform worn by practitioners of martial arts like Karate and Judo is called a ______.
questions.explanation: Derived from the Japanese word 'Keikogi', meaning training gear.

questions.text: Which of the following are scoring zones on a standard dartboard? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: The dartboard consists of the inner bullseye, outer bullseye, triple ring, and double ring. There is no 'Foul Zone'.

questions.text: Match the major North American sports leagues to their respective sports:
questions.explanation: These acronyms stand for National Hockey League, Major League Baseball, and Major League Soccer.

questions.text: How many players make up a standard Rugby Union team on the pitch?
questions.explanation: Rugby Union is played with 15 players per team, whereas Rugby League is played with 13.

questions.text: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) officially recognizes chess as a sport.
questions.explanation: Yes, the IOC recognizes the World Chess Federation (FIDE) and classifies chess as a 'mind sport'.

questions.text: In competitive tennis, a score of zero for a player is referred to as '______'.
questions.explanation: It is widely believed to have originated from the French word for egg ('l'oeuf'), resembling a zero shape.

questions.text: Assertion: The 'Fosbury Flop' revolutionized the high jump event. Reason: Dick Fosbury realized that jumping over the bar backwards allowed his center of mass to pass under the bar, making higher jumps possible.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. Fosbury introduced this technique at the 1968 Olympics, changing the sport forever.

questions.text: Which of the following positions are part of a baseball team's defense? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Shortstops, catchers, and pitchers play baseball. Quarterbacks are in American football, and liberos are in volleyball.

questions.text: Match the legendary sports venues to their primary sport:
questions.explanation: These are some of the most hallowed and historic grounds in their respective sports.

questions.text: In volleyball, the defensive specialist who wears a different colored jersey from their teammates is called the:
questions.explanation: The libero is a back-row defensive specialist who cannot block or attack the ball above the net height.

questions.text: In the game of snooker, potting the black ball awards the player 7 points.
questions.explanation: The black ball is the highest scoring ball on the table, worth 7 points.

questions.text: The piece of equipment passed between runners during an athletics relay race is called a ______.
questions.explanation: A relay baton is a smooth, hollow tube passed from one teammate to the next.

questions.text: Assertion: The introduction of the three-point line dramatically changed NBA strategy. Reason: It incentivized players to shoot from further away by offering a higher point reward.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. The modern 'pace and space' era is entirely built around the mathematical advantage of the three-point shot.

questions.text: A standard feather shuttlecock used in professional badminton is made using exactly 16 goose or duck feathers.
questions.explanation: Professional shuttlecocks are tightly regulated and must contain exactly 16 feathers fixed into a cork base.

questions.text: The maximum score a gymnast could achieve under the perfect scoring system popularized by Nadia Comăneci was a ______.
questions.explanation: The 'Perfect 10' was the hallmark of Olympic gymnastics before the scoring system was changed to an open-ended code in 2006.

questions.text: Which of these are the four major championships (Majors) in men's professional golf? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: The four majors are The Masters, PGA Championship (not listed), US Open, and The Open Championship. The Players and Ryder Cup are not majors.

questions.text: Match the colors of the Olympic rings to the regions they traditionally represent (as originally designed by Pierre de Coubertin):
questions.explanation: While the IOC no longer strictly associates specific colors with specific continents, this was Coubertin's original design intent.

questions.text: What does 'UFC' stand for in the world of mixed martial arts?
questions.explanation: The Ultimate Fighting Championship is the largest and premier MMA promotion company in the world.

questions.text: The sport of squash is played on a table using a small celluloid ball.
questions.explanation: Table tennis (ping pong) is played on a table. Squash is played in an enclosed court where players hit a rubber ball against the walls.

questions.text: In a standard game of ten-pin bowling, rolling 12 consecutive strikes results in a perfect score of ______ points.
questions.explanation: A perfect game consists of a strike in the first nine frames, and three strikes in the tenth frame, totaling 300 points.

questions.text: Assertion: Gymnasts and weightlifters heavily coat their hands in white powder before competing. Reason: The powder is magnesium carbonate, which absorbs sweat and improves their grip on the apparatus.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. Gymnastic chalk is critical for safety to prevent slipping due to sweaty palms.

questions.text: Which of these track and field events are classified as 'throwing' events? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Shot put, discus, and hammer throw are throwing events. Pole vault is a jumping event. Javelin is a throwing event.

questions.text: Match the specific equipment to the sport that uses it:
questions.explanation: These are the fundamental objects being struck in these respective sports.

questions.text: Which country is renowned for producing the vast majority of the world's elite marathon and long-distance runners?
questions.explanation: Kenya (along with Ethiopia) is globally famous for producing world-record-holding endurance runners like Eliud Kipchoge.

questions.text: Surfing and Skateboarding made their official Olympic debuts at the 2020 Tokyo Olympic Games.
questions.explanation: Both action sports were introduced in Tokyo (held in 2021) to appeal to younger audiences.

questions.text: Tom Brady, widely considered the greatest NFL quarterback, won his final Super Bowl playing for the Tampa Bay ______.
questions.explanation: After 20 years with the Patriots, Brady moved to the Buccaneers and won Super Bowl LV.

questions.text: Assertion: Sprinters use starting blocks for races like the 100m dash. Reason: The blocks give them a rigid surface to push off against, maximizing their explosive forward acceleration.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. Starting blocks are essential for generating maximum horizontal force at the gunshot.

questions.text: Which of these football (soccer) clubs are located in London? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Arsenal, Chelsea, and Tottenham are based in London. Manchester United and Liverpool are based in their namesake cities.

questions.text: Match the recent Summer Olympic Games to their host city:
questions.explanation: These cities successfully hosted the massive international multi-sport events in those years.

questions.text: Who holds the record for the most wickets taken in Test Cricket history?

questions.text: In a standard game of golf, a 'bogey' means the player scored one stroke over par on a hole.
questions.explanation: Par is the expected number of strokes. One under is a birdie, one over is a bogey.

questions.text: The official height of a regulation basketball hoop from the floor is exactly ______ feet.
questions.explanation: James Naismith set the peach baskets at 10 feet in 1891, and that standard has remained in the NBA and FIBA ever since.
